rubric.target_patient: A postmenopausal or premenopausal adult with histologically confirmed, ER and/or PR-positive, HER2-negative invasive breast cancer who has completed definitive surgery (breast-conserving or mastectomy with negative margins) and standard neoadjuvant or adjuvant chemotherapy, and who is classified as high-risk by nodal status and/or genomic assay: node-negative with a tumor ≥2cm and Oncotype DX RS>25, 1-3 positive nodes with RS>25 or Grade III histology, ≥4 positive nodes, or any node-positive disease after neoadjuvant chemo. She is planned for standard adjuvant endocrine therapy and is being considered for randomization to add one year of everolimus or placebo to that endocrine backbone.
rubric.site_burden: No schedule of assessments was provided in the record, so burden is inferred rather than read from protocol. This is a long-horizon (up to 5-year IDFS follow-up), placebo-controlled, blinded oral drug trial layered on top of standard adjuvant endocrine therapy (tamoxifen, an aromatase inhibitor, and/or ovarian suppression with goserelin/leuprolide). Running it plausibly requires: reliable long-term patient retention and follow-up infrastructure; a research pharmacy or investigational drug service able to dispense and track blinded oral everolimus/placebo alongside multiple standard endocrine agents; toxicity monitoring capacity for mTOR-inhibitor-specific adverse events (stomatitis, non-infectious pneumonitis, hyperglycemia, infection risk) over a full year of dosing; and coordination with pathology/genomic testing (Oncotype DX) to confirm eligibility. As a 1459-site NCI cooperative group (SWOG) trial, it also implies routine NCTN regulatory, data-reporting, and cooperative-group compliance infrastructure rather than a sponsor-managed single-site monitoring model.

criteria.requirement: The investigator must be a medical oncologist or hematologist-oncologist managing adjuvant systemic therapy for HR-positive/HER2-negative breast cancer.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy code for the physician includes Medical Oncology or Hematology/Oncology.
criteria.derived_from: eligibility: patients must have HR+/HER2- invasive breast carcinoma for whom standard adjuvant endocrine therapy is planned

criteria.requirement: The site must be affiliated with SWOG or another NCI National Clinical Trials Network group, since this is a network-sponsored cooperative group protocol rather than an industry-run single trial.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site or investigator is listed as an NCTN/SWOG member site, or has a prior SWOG-sponsored trial listed as an investigator on ClinicalTrials.gov.
criteria.derived_from: sponsor: SWOG Cancer Research Network (NETWORK); 1459-site cooperative group footprint

criteria.requirement: The investigator needs demonstrated expertise sequencing adjuvant/neoadjuvant chemotherapy with extended adjuvant endocrine therapy in high-risk HR+/HER2- breast cancer.
criteria.satisfies_if: PubMed record includes authorship on adjuvant breast cancer endocrine or chemotherapy trials, or the physician is listed as an investigator on a similar SWOG/NCTN breast cancer trial in ClinicalTrials.gov.
criteria.derived_from: eligibility criteria requiring completed adjuvant/neoadjuvant chemotherapy and planned standard adjuvant endocrine therapy

criteria.requirement: The site physician must be experienced monitoring and managing everolimus-associated toxicities (stomatitis, pneumonitis, hyperglycemia, infection risk) in a non-metastatic, curative-intent setting.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician self-reports prior clinical experience prescribing or managing mTOR inhibitors (e.g., everolimus, temsirolimus) in oncology patients.
criteria.derived_from: intervention list includes everolimus PO given against placebo for one year

criteria.requirement: The practice must regularly see high-risk HR+/HER2- breast cancer patients who are node-positive or have high Oncotype DX recurrence scores, post-chemotherapy, in sufficient volume to recruit for this trial.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician self-reports an annual volume of high-risk HR+/HER2- breast cancer patients meeting the trial's nodal/Oncotype thresholds, or Medicare claims show a breast cancer oncology practice volume consistent with this subpopulation as a floor estimate.
criteria.derived_from: eligibility tiers based on nodal status (pN1mi, 1-3 nodes, 4+ nodes) and Oncotype DX RS>25 thresholds

criteria.requirement: The site must have routine access to Oncotype DX recurrence score testing as standard of care, since eligibility hinges on RS thresholds.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician self-reports routine ordering of Oncotype DX testing as part of standard practice for node-negative/low-node-positive breast cancer.
criteria.derived_from: eligibility requiring Oncotype DX RS>25 completed as standard of care

criteria.requirement: The site must be able to retain and follow enrolled patients for up to 5 years post-registration to capture the IDFS primary endpoint, including a full year of blinded drug administration and toxicity monitoring.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician/site self-reports infrastructure for multi-year oncology trial follow-up (dedicated research coordinator continuity, long-term EDC data entry, patient retention protocols); no schedule of assessments was available so this burden is inferred from the outcome timeframe rather than confirmed against a protocol document.
criteria.derived_from: primary outcome: Invasive Disease-Free Survival up to 5 years post registration; note in record that schedule of assessments was not available

criteria.requirement: The site pharmacy must be able to dispense and track blinded oral study drug (everolimus/placebo) alongside multiple possible standard endocrine agents (anastrozole, letrozole, exemestane, tamoxifen) and injectable ovarian suppression agents (goserelin, leuprolide).
criteria.satisfies_if: Site self-reports a research pharmacy or investigational drug service capable of blinded oral drug dispensing and injectable hormonal agent administration.
criteria.derived_from: intervention list of 8 drugs including a placebo-controlled blinded everolimus arm alongside multiple endocrine backbone options

criteria.requirement: The investigator should have prior experience serving as a site PI or co-investigator on phase III adjuvant breast cancer or cooperative-group oncology trials.
criteria.satisfies_if: ClinicalTrials.gov record lists the physician as an investigator on at least one completed or active phase III oncology trial.
criteria.derived_from: trial phase PHASE3; large multi-site (1459-site) cooperative group design

criteria.requirement: A track record of research-related sponsor payments indicative of active clinical trial conduct strengthens fit for running this multi-drug interventional protocol.
criteria.satisfies_if: Open Payments record shows research-category payments attributed to the physician or affiliated site.
criteria.derived_from: sponsor-funded, multi-drug, placebo-controlled interventional design requiring research infrastructure and funding relationships

excluded_screening: Absolute Neutrophil Count \u2265 1,500/mL \u2014 a per-patient lab eligibility threshold, not a site characteristic
Hemoglobin \u2265 9 g/dL \u2014 per-patient lab threshold checked at enrollment
Bilirubin \u2264 1.5 mg/dL (or \u2264 3.0 if Gilbert syndrome) \u2014 per-patient organ function screening
ALT/AST \u2264 1.5x IULN \u2014 per-patient liver function screening
Serum creatinine \u2264 IULN \u2014 per-patient renal function screening
Zubrod performance status 0-2 \u2014 per-patient functional status at enrollment
HgA1C \u2264 7% for known diabetics \u2014 per-patient metabolic control check tied to everolimus hyperglycemia risk, not a site capability
Not pregnant or nursing \u2014 per-patient screening requirement
Contraception requirements (non-hormonal methods, celibacy, sterilization definitions) \u2014 per-patient consent/behavioral requirement
No use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors/inducers within 14 days \u2014 per-patient medication reconciliation, not a site-level capability
No GI impairment/absorption disorders \u2014 per-patient medical history exclusion
No investigational drug within 28 days prior \u2014 per-patient washout requirement
